title: katherinea rotundata
intro: Katherinea rotundata, also known as the Indian snake root or Katerra, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. Katherinea rotundata is often used in herbal preparations like decoctions and infusions, and its roots are also used in traditional remedies to treat snake bites and other infections.
